methods.description: Process for extracting proteins from 10-day-old seedlings for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: Protein extraction from 10-d-old seedlings was conducted by grinding them in a mixer mill and adding extraction buffer.
steps.searchable_summary: Grind seedlings and add extraction buffer for protein extraction.

steps.description: Extracts were incubated on ice for 15 min and centrifuged for 3 min at 14,000 rpm.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate extracts on ice and centrifuge.

methods.description: Procedure for separating proteins by SDS-PAGE and transferring them to a membrane for detection.

steps.description: Protein extracts were boiled at 95°C with SDS loading buffer and loaded on a 10% gel followed by SDS-PAGE under reducing conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Boil protein extracts with SDS buffer and perform SDS-PAGE.

steps.description: After blotting to a PVDF membrane, the membrane was blocked in 5% milk in TBST.
steps.searchable_summary: Blot proteins to PVDF membrane and block with milk in TBST.

steps.description: The membrane was probed with anti-GFP B-2 antibody, washed with TBST, treated with the secondary antibody, and detected using chemiluminescence.
steps.searchable_summary: Probe membrane with antibodies and detect using chemiluminescence.

methods.description: Procedure for detecting proteins using ConA labeling on a PVDF membrane.

steps.description: The SDS gel was blotted to PVDF membrane and labeled according to manufacturer's recommendations.
steps.searchable_summary: Blot SDS gel to PVDF membrane and label with ConA.

methods.description: Procedure for assessing protein amounts using Coomassie staining.

steps.description: The reducing SDS gel was stained with Coomassie Brilliant Blue R250 solution and destained with destaining solution.
steps.searchable_summary: Stain and destain SDS gel with Coomassie Brilliant Blue.

methods.description: Procedure for deglycosylating proteins using EndoH digestion.

steps.description: EndoH digestion was performed according to the manufacturer's instructions under reducing conditions.
